name: IT Onboarding — New User Setup
description: Standard procedure for creating a new user account in Active Directory and configuring their workstation.

steps.description: Open Active Directory Users and Computers (dsa.msc)
steps.hint: Press Win+R, type dsa.msc and hit Enter. Or find it in Administrative Tools.

steps.description: Navigate to the correct Organizational Unit (OU) for the new user
steps.hint: Expand the domain tree in the left pane. Right-click the target OU.

steps.description: Create new user account — right-click OU and select New > User
steps.hint: Right-click the Users OU or target OU, hover New, then click User.

steps.description: Enter first name, last name, and User logon name, then click Next
steps.hint: Fill in all required fields. The UPN suffix should match the domain.

steps.description: Set the initial password and configure password options
steps.hint: For service accounts: check 'Password never expires'. For users: check 'User must change password at next logon'.

steps.description: Review account summary and click Finish to create the account
steps.hint: Verify the settings are correct before finishing.

steps.description: Add user to appropriate security groups (e.g. Domain Users, VPN-Users)
steps.hint: Right-click the new user, Properties, Member Of tab, Add groups.

steps.description: Verify account properties and enable the account if disabled
steps.hint: Check that the account is enabled (no red X on icon). Verify email, department, phone.
